title: Bible Facts & Trivia

facts.question: How many books are in the Bible?
facts.answer: 66 books - 39 in the Old Testament and 27 in the New Testament.

facts.question: How many chapters are in the Bible?
facts.answer: 1,189 chapters total - 929 in the Old Testament and 260 in the New Testament.

facts.question: How many verses are in the Bible?
facts.answer: 31,102 verses total - 23,145 in the Old Testament and 7,957 in the New Testament.

facts.question: How many words are in the Bible?
facts.answer: Approximately 783,000 words - 602,000 in the Old Testament and 181,000 in the New Testament.

facts.question: What is the longest book in the Bible?
facts.answer: Psalms - with 150 chapters and 2,461 verses.

facts.question: What is the shortest book in the Bible?
facts.answer: 3 John - with only 1 chapter and 14 verses (or 2 John with 13 verses depending on translation).

facts.question: What is the longest chapter in the Bible?
facts.answer: Psalm 119 - with 176 verses. It's also an acrostic poem with 22 stanzas, one for each letter of the Hebrew alphabet.

facts.question: What is the shortest chapter in the Bible?
facts.answer: Psalm 117 - with only 2 verses. It's also the middle chapter of the Bible.

facts.question: What is the longest verse in the Bible?
facts.answer: Esther 8:9 - with 90 words in English (or 43 words in Hebrew). It describes the king's decree being sent throughout the Persian empire.

facts.question: What is the shortest verse in the Bible?
facts.answer: John 11:35 - 'Jesus wept.' (2 words in English, 16 Greek letters).

facts.question: How many people wrote the Bible?
facts.answer: About 40 different authors over approximately 1,500 years (from around 1400 BC to 100 AD).

facts.question: Who wrote the most books in the Bible?
facts.answer: The Apostle Paul wrote 13 books (or 14 if Hebrews is included).

facts.question: Who wrote the most chapters in the Bible?
facts.answer: Moses wrote the most chapters - 50 in Genesis, 40 in Exodus, 27 in Leviticus, 36 in Numbers, and 34 in Deuteronomy = 187 chapters total.

facts.question: What language was the Old Testament written in?
facts.answer: Primarily Hebrew, with small portions in Aramaic (Ezra 4:8-6:18, 7:12-26; Daniel 2:4-7:28; and Jeremiah 10:11).

facts.question: What language was the New Testament written in?
facts.answer: Koine Greek - the common language of the Mediterranean world at that time.

facts.question: What are the first five books of the Bible called?
facts.answer: The Torah (Hebrew) or Pentateuch (Greek) - Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy. Also known as the Books of Moses.

facts.question: How long did it take Moses to write the Pentateuch?
facts.answer: About 40 years, from approximately 1445-1405 BC, during Israel's wilderness wandering.

facts.question: Who is mentioned most in the Old Testament?
facts.answer: David is mentioned 1,118 times by name, more than any other person in the Old Testament.

facts.question: What is the oldest book in the Bible?
facts.answer: Job is considered the oldest book, likely written around 2000-1800 BC, before Moses.

facts.question: How many prophecies about Jesus are in the Old Testament?
facts.answer: Over 300 prophecies about the Messiah, written hundreds of years before Jesus' birth.

facts.question: How many years did Jesus minister on earth?
facts.answer: Approximately 3.5 years, from His baptism to His crucifixion and resurrection.

facts.question: How many miracles did Jesus perform?
facts.answer: The Gospels record about 37 specific miracles, though John 21:25 says Jesus did many more not written down.

facts.question: How many parables did Jesus tell?
facts.answer: About 40 parables are recorded in the Gospels.

facts.question: How many apostles did Jesus have?
facts.answer: 12 original apostles, with Matthias replacing Judas Iscariot. Paul later became known as the 'Apostle to the Gentiles.'

facts.question: What is the last book of the Bible?
facts.answer: Revelation - written by the Apostle John around 95 AD while exiled on the island of Patmos.

facts.question: What is the middle verse of the Bible?
facts.answer: Psalm 118:8 - 'It is better to trust in the LORD than to put confidence in man.' (Note: In some versions, this varies slightly)

facts.question: How many times does 'fear not' appear in the Bible?
facts.answer: 365 times - one for every day of the year!

facts.question: What is the most common name in the Bible?
facts.answer: Zacharias/Zechariah appears 41 times. However, if you count variations, names like John, Simon, and Judas appear frequently.

facts.question: How many animals are mentioned in the Bible?
facts.answer: Over 120 different species of animals are mentioned throughout Scripture.

facts.question: What is the most quoted Old Testament book in the New Testament?
facts.answer: Psalms is quoted over 80 times in the New Testament, more than any other Old Testament book.

facts.question: How old was Methuselah when he died?
facts.answer: 969 years old - the oldest person mentioned in the Bible (Genesis 5:27).

facts.question: What is the most frequently mentioned number in the Bible?
facts.answer: The number 7 appears over 700 times, symbolizing completeness or perfection.

facts.question: How many people were raised from the dead in the Bible?
facts.answer: At least 10 people were raised from the dead in Scripture, including Jesus, Lazarus, and various others raised by prophets and apostles.

facts.question: What was the first complete printed Bible?
facts.answer: The Gutenberg Bible, printed by Johannes Gutenberg around 1455 in Mainz, Germany.

facts.question: What was the first English Bible?
facts.answer: Wycliffe's Bible, translated by John Wycliffe and completed in 1382.

facts.question: When was the King James Version published?
facts.answer: 1611 - commissioned by King James I of England and still one of the most popular translations.

facts.question: How many languages has the Bible been translated into?
facts.answer: The complete Bible has been translated into over 700 languages, with portions available in over 3,500 languages.

facts.question: What is the best-selling book of all time?
facts.answer: The Bible - with over 5 billion copies sold and distributed worldwide.

daily_facts: The Bible was written over a period of 1,500 years by 40 different authors.
The longest word in the Bible is 'Mahershalalhashbaz' (Isaiah 8:1).
The Bible never uses the word 'Trinity' - the concept is developed from various passages.
There are 1,189 chapters in the Bible.
Esther is the only book in the Bible that doesn't mention God by name.
The word 'Christian' appears only 3 times in the Bible.
Jesus is mentioned over 1,200 times in the New Testament.
Psalm 117 is the shortest chapter and also the middle chapter.
The book of Genesis covers more time than all the other books combined.
